mood.label: CAUTIOUSLY-RISK-ON
mood.summary: Markets are digesting yesterday's strong rally fueled by a potential US-Iran deal and falling oil prices. Eyes are now on the FOMC meeting which begins today, creating a wait-and-see approach.

indicesNarrative: US indices saw a significant rally on Monday, driven by optimism over a US-Iran peace framework which sent oil prices tumbling. The Nasdaq led the charge with a more than 3% gain, indicating a strong risk-on sentiment for tech and growth stocks. [6, 7] However, futures are mixed this morning, suggesting a pause as traders await the Federal Reserve's policy meeting.

sectorsThemes.name: Technology
sectorsThemes.note: Outperformed yesterday on broad market rally and AI enthusiasm. [4]

sectorsThemes.name: Energy
sectorsThemes.note: Under pressure as oil prices fell sharply on news of a potential US-Iran deal. [13]

sectorsThemes.name: Financials
sectorsThemes.note: Showed strength last week, part of a broader rotation into value sectors. [6]

sectorsThemes.name: Materials
sectorsThemes.note: Was the top-performing sector last week, benefiting from the rotation to cyclicals. [6]

sectorsNarrative: While technology stocks led Monday's rally, the broader theme over the past week has been a rotation from mega-cap growth into value and cyclical sectors like Materials and Financials. [5, 6] This trend suggests improving market breadth, though the tech sector remains a key driver of short-term momentum.
industryNarrative: Chip stocks were notable leaders in Monday's session. [2] Airline stocks also saw significant gains as the sharp drop in crude oil prices points to lower fuel costs. [8]

movers.why: High relative volume pre-market gainer without a clear news catalyst.
Pre-market gainer on high volume, appears to be moving with broader tech and semiconductor strength.
Continued upward momentum following its blockbuster IPO last week.
Micro-cap stock with extreme volatility, common for this group which often faces delisting or dilution risks.
movers.traderLens: Avoid/fade for most styles due to extreme risk.

movers.why: Negative reaction to its after-hours earnings report from June 15.
Another volatile micro-cap stock showing significant pre-market weakness.
movers.traderLens: Avoid/fade due to high risk and unpredictability.

watchlist.note: Key leader in the AI space, which saw a broad rally yesterday. Goldman Sachs reiterated a Buy rating recently. [10]
Beneficiary of general risk-on sentiment, but showing relative softness compared to other tech giants.
Continues to be a core holding, watch for relative strength against the Nasdaq.
Another key AI player, watch for follow-through momentum from yesterday's tech rally.
Trading constructively, could benefit from a continued broad market rally.
Also part of the big-tech rally, looking for signs of continued institutional buying.
Participated in yesterday's rally, but has been a relative laggard among mega-caps.
Semiconductor peer to NVDA, will likely move with the broader chip industry.
High-beta name in the AI space, can have outsized moves on strong tech days.
Appears to be in a consolidation phase after a period of weakness. [26]
Cybersecurity leader, watch for sector-specific catalysts.
Moves as a proxy for Bitcoin, which is currently holding steady.

traderLens.name: Minervini
traderLens.view: Market is in a confirmed uptrend after yesterday's powerful move, but caution is warranted ahead of the Fed. Focus on A-grade setups only.

traderLens.name: Ted Zhang
traderLens.view: Observing a rotation from tech into value and cyclicals over the past week, although tech had a strong rebound yesterday. Watch to see which trend persists.

traderLens.name: Clement Ang
traderLens.view: Yesterday was a strong day, but the market is entering a period of uncertainty with the FOMC meeting. Best to wait for the market to digest the Fed's statement before committing new capital.

traderLens.name: SRxTrades
traderLens.view: Look for clean pivots and volume confirmation. Chasing gaps after a 3% Nasdaq run is a low-probability entry.

traderLens.name: Jeff
traderLens.view: The plan is to wait for the Fed. Do not deviate. Stick to the rules and protect capital.

traderLens.name: Qullamaggie
traderLens.view: Yesterday's move was powerful, creating potential for follow-through momentum. Look for strong stocks gapping up on high volume and breaking out of clean bases.

traderLens.name: Composite
traderLens.view: Sit on hands and observe. The major catalyst is the FOMC meeting. Let the market react to the news, then identify the new leadership and setups for the coming days.

alert: FOMC meeting begins today; expect potential volatility around the statement release tomorrow.

news.headline: Stock Rally Pauses as Traders Await Fed's First Move Under New Chair
US Stocks Rallied Monday on Hopes of US-Iran Peace Deal
Oil Prices Tumble as Potential Iran Deal Eases Supply Concerns
Bank of Japan Hikes Interest Rate to 1.0%, Highest Since 1995
Asian Markets Mixed, European Stocks Rise in Early Trade
